canto_title: The Celestial Ladder and the Prophecy of the Corrupt Church

scenes.summary: Beatrice warns Dante that her beauty is too intense for a mortal to behold directly without being destroyed like Semele.
Dante reflects on his internal state and his willingness to obey his celestial guide.
Dante observes a luminous ladder of light descending through the crystalline sphere of the universe.
Dante compares the movement of the descending lights to the natural behavior of the sun's rays at dawn.
Dante perceives the intensity of the light but chooses to suppress his desire to ask questions.
Beatrice perceives Dante's restraint and Dante asks why the divine music is silent in this sphere.
Beatrice explains that the silence is due to her lack of laughter and that divine love governs the spheres.
Dante asks if Beatrice's specific role in this sphere was predestined for her alone.
The divine Light responds, describing how God's essence penetrates its being and elevates it.
The Light explains that the highest mysteries of the Seraphim are beyond mortal understanding and warns Dante not to probe further.
Dante ceases his questioning and turns his attention to a new voice to ask his identity.
The new speaker delivers a prophecy regarding the rise of a deformed power in the region of Catria.
The speaker describes his former life of contemplative prayer in a peaceful monastery.
The speaker reveals his identity as Peter Damian and mentions his time in the house of the Virgin Mary.
The speaker laments the corruption of the clergy, where leaders and followers are indistinguishable and deceitful.
The descending lights gather and emit a thunderous cry that overwhelms Dante's senses.
